notes: Livermore has no existing page/baseline in this repo, so this is a from-scratch research pass, cross-checked primarily against the Livermore Area Recreation and Park District (LARPD, larpd.org) -- the city's actual parks & rec authority (Livermore itself is not the courts operator; LARPD is a special district that runs all public parks/rec facilities in Livermore). Pickleheads' own synthesized summary states '4 pickleball locations with 22 courts' in Livermore, listing: Livermore Downs Park (4), Robert Livermore Community Center (6), Livermore Downs (4), May Nissen Park (8) -- 4+6+4+8=22, confirming the math. HOWEVER, 'Livermore Downs' and 'Livermore Downs Park' are the same real-world facility (2100 Paseo Laguna Seco): every non-Pickleheads source (LARPD's own park page, Tri-Valley Pickleball Club, DailyPB, Bounce, HAQ Pickle, teachme.to) describes exactly ONE 4-court park at this address, never two. This is the same stale-duplicate-listing pattern already documented in this repo for Berkeley (Ocean View Park) and Pleasanton (Clubsport/Bay Club) -- Pickleheads appears to carry an old page under a shortened name ('Livermore Downs') alongside the current one ('Livermore Downs Park') and both get counted toward its location/court totals. Only ONE venue entry is included below for this park. Separately, LARPD's own official rentals page lists a THIRD outdoor venue, Jack Williams Park (2 tennis/pickleball combo courts, drop-in only), that does not appear anywhere on Pickleheads -- a real gap in the opposite direction, included here as venue #4. Net effect: the 4 venues below are Livermore Downs Park + May Nissen Park + Robert Livermore Community Center (all deduped/reconciled from Pickleheads' 4 listed pages) plus Jack Williams Park (LARPD-only, not a Pickleheads page at all) -- a coincidentally-identical count of 4 but a materially different set of venues than Pickleheads' own '4 locations'. No numeric Google Maps CID could be confirmed for any venue with the tools available this session (Google Maps searches in the browser tool did not resolve to place results), so every googleMapsUrl is left null rather than guessing or using a lower-quality URL format. No Pickleheads star rating surfaced for any venue via search, so pickleheadsRating is null throughout. lat/lon for all 4 venues come from OpenStreetMap/Nominatim named-place lookups (a real polygon/POI match for each park by name, not a rough city-centroid guess), not from a hand-confirmed Pickleheads pin -- reasonably precise but noted per-venue as sourced this way rather than site-standard-confirmed.

venues.name: Livermore Downs Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets (per Pickleheads/Tri-Valley Pickleball Club); LARPD's own rentals page lists this park as drop-in only with no rental fee tier at all, unlike May Nissen -- consistent with these being simple free dedicated courts outside LARPD's paid reservation system
venues.hours: Not specified (LARPD states drop-in only; no specific daily open/close hours found)
venues.weather: Far eastern Tri-Valley wine country -- Livermore runs hotter and drier than the rest of the Bay Area, with summer highs regularly in the 90s-100F+ and little coastal fog relief; spring and fall are the most comfortable months to play, winters are mild and wetter.
venues.notes: 4.5-acre neighborhood park; 'four (4) new pickleball courts' per LARPD's own park page (this park was built out with pickleball as a named amenity, unlike May Nissen which converted/added courts to an older multi-sport park). Pickleheads carries a duplicate page under the shortened name 'Livermore Downs' (no 'Park') describing the identical 4 outdoor courts/permanent nets/free -- see top-level notes; treated as the same venue, not a second entry. Minor address variance across sources: LARPD's own park page says 2100 Paseo Laguna Seco / 94550; Tri-Valley Pickleball Club, Yelp, and several aggregators say 2101 Paseo Laguna Seco / 94551. Used LARPD's own figure as primary since it is the official source, but this is a trivial house-number/zip discrepancy, not a core-fact conflict, so it doesn't affect the confirmed status. No source stated whether the courts are lighted, so lights is left 'Not specified' rather than assumed.

venues.name: May Nissen Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- Pickleheads describes a mix of asphalt, hard, and concrete across the 8 courts. Per LARPD's own rental page: 4 courts (1A, 1B, 2A, 2B) have LARPD-installed permanent nets; the other 4 (3A, 3B, 4A, 4B) are lined for pickleball but LARPD does not provide nets there -- players must bring their own.
venues.netType: Permanent nets on courts 1A/1B/2A/2B; lined-only, bring-your-own-net on courts 3A/3B/4A/4B
venues.price: Free for walk-up/drop-in play. LARPD also offers paid advance reservations: $10 per court per hour for the net-equipped courts (1A, 1B, 2A, 2B), $5 per court per hour for the lined/no-net courts (3A, 3B, 4A, 4B). Reservation fees are non-refundable; LARPD asks for five business days' notice to process a rental request.
venues.hours: Not specified (open for drop-in play; reservation windows subject to LARPD's rental process, no fixed daily hours found)
amenities: Adjacent to May Nissen Swim Center and the Rincon branch library
venues.weather: Far eastern Tri-Valley wine country -- Livermore runs hotter and drier than the rest of the Bay Area, with summer highs regularly in the 90s-100F+ and little coastal fog relief; spring and fall are the most comfortable months to play, winters are mild and wetter.
venues.notes: Court count (8, split 4+4) is unanimous across LARPD's own rental page, Pickleheads, Places2Play, Tri-Valley Pickleball Club, and Pickleballify -- strong corroboration. The one nuance worth flagging: Pickleheads, Tri-Valley Pickleball Club, and Pickleballify all describe the courts simply as 'free to play' / first-come-first-served with Pickleballify explicitly stating no reservations are accepted, while LARPD's own official rental page explicitly puts May Nissen on its paid per-court-hour reservation schedule. These aren't actually contradictory -- LARPD's own page states the courts are 'available for drop-in use and are available to rent,' i.e. free walk-up play when a court isn't reserved, with the paid option layered on top for anyone who wants a guaranteed time. Recorded as 'Mixed' rather than treating it as an unresolved conflict. Zip code varies 94550 (LARPD, this record) vs 94551 (several third-party listings) -- minor, not a core-fact conflict.

venues.name: Robert Livermore Community Center
venues.surface: Indoor wood gymnasium floor, shared multi-use with basketball and volleyball (LARPD's own site describes a 2024/2025 gym-floor refurbishment covering all three sports); permanent pickleball lines, portable nets brought in for play.
venues.price: LARPD's own current (2026 season) adult drop-in program page states $7 per visit for adults 18+, $6 for seniors 55+, with multi-visit punch passes also sold (5-visit $33/$28, 10-visit $65/$55, 20-visit $128/$108, expiring each calendar year). Pickleheads and Bounce both instead state a flat $6 drop-in / $5 senior figure -- see notes; recorded here per LARPD's own current page as the most authoritative and most recently dated source.
venues.hours: Adult drop-in pickleball: Tue & Thu 11:30am-2:30pm, Wed 6-9pm, year-round excluding district holidays (per LARPD's own 2026 program page)
venues.weather: Far eastern Tri-Valley wine country -- Livermore runs hotter and drier than the rest of the Bay Area, with summer highs regularly in the 90s-100F+ and little coastal fog relief; spring and fall are the most comfortable months to play, winters are mild and wetter (indoor courts, so less relevant here than at the outdoor venues).
venues.notes: Left confirmed:false because the indoor court count is genuinely disputed and LARPD's own official page never states a specific number: Pickleheads and Bounce.game both say 6 (identical wording -- 'permanent lines, portable nets available' -- strongly suggesting Bounce mirrors Pickleheads rather than being a fully independent count); Places2Play separately lists this same facility (as 'Livermore Area Recreation And Park District,' 4444 East Ave) with 3 indoor courts and a visibly stale fee/schedule ($4 drop-in, Tue/Thu 11:30am-1:30pm) that doesn't match LARPD's current 2026 program page at all, suggesting Places2Play's entry predates a schedule/price change; Tri-Valley Pickleball Club hedges outright with 'three to six indoor courts,' which reads like the club itself knows the number varies by how many portable nets get set up on the shared gym floor for a given session. Price also varies: this record uses LARPD's own current $7/$6 program page (freshest, explicitly dated for 2026), while Pickleheads/Bounce state $6/$5 and an older general search turned up a $4/session figure that looks superseded. Existence and indoor status are not in doubt (LARPD official program pages, a 20th-anniversary write-up in the Pleasanton Weekly, Yelp, and every aggregator checked all agree this is a real, active indoor pickleball program) -- only the exact court count and current price are unresolved, which is why confirmed is false rather than the venue being in question. Address note: LARPD's own 'Robert Livermore Gymnasium' page separately states '4446 East Avenue' (vs. 4444 used everywhere else, including LARPD's own adult-pickleball program pages) -- almost certainly just the specific gym building's number within the same community-center complex, not a different location, but noted for completeness.

venues.name: Jack Williams Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, shared tennis/pickleball combo lines (LARPD's own park page notes the courts were resurfaced in 2002 -- among the older court surfaces of the venues checked in this file)
venues.hours: Not specified (LARPD states drop-in only; no specific daily open/close hours found)
venues.weather: Far eastern Tri-Valley wine country -- Livermore runs hotter and drier than the rest of the Bay Area, with summer highs regularly in the 90s-100F+ and little coastal fog relief; spring and fall are the most comfortable months to play, winters are mild and wetter.
venues.notes: NOT on Pickleheads at all -- this venue does not appear on Pickleheads' Livermore city index or in any per-venue search, despite being an official, currently-operating LARPD facility. Included here because the task scope is real public pickleball venues in Livermore, not just what Pickleheads happens to list (see top-level notes). LARPD's own rentals page and dedicated park page both explicitly describe 'two (2) tennis/pickleball combo courts - drop-in only' and 'not lighted.' Independently corroborated by tennisround.com (a tennis-specific court directory unrelated to the Pickleheads/aggregator ecosystem), which lists '2 public tennis courts, not lighted' at this same park -- matching LARPD's count and lighting exactly, though tennisround.com describes them only as tennis courts and doesn't itself confirm pickleball striping (LARPD is the source for the combo/pickleball designation). tennisround.com gives a different street address for the same park, 1955 Pulsar Ave, vs. LARPD's 2041 Neptune Rd -- most likely a second street frontage/entrance to the same park block rather than a real location conflict, but flagged since it wasn't resolved definitively. A Yelp reviewer separately mentions 'there also appears to be a tennis court' at this park, consistent with but not a strong independent confirmation of the LARPD figures. No net type stated by any source for this specific park, so left 'Not specified' rather than assumed from how other LARPD combo courts are typically equipped.
